title: Bullockia setiflora
intro: Bullockia setiflora, also known as Setiflora, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its various health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of fever, colds, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Bullockia setiflora include alkaloids, phenolic acids, and flavonoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Bullockia setiflora is commonly prepared as a tea, infusion, or decoction, and is used to treat a range of health conditions.
taxonomy: Bullockia setiflora is a species within the genus Bullockia, which is classified under the family Rubiaceae. This family is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as the coffee or madder family. The genus Bullockia is a group of flowering plants native to tropical Africa. The classification of Bullockia setiflora places it within the order Gentianales, a group of flowering plants that also includes the families Apocynaceae and Loganiaceae. The exact classification of Bullockia setiflora may vary depending on the source, but it is generally accepted to be within the above-mentioned family and order.
